expectations.text: A faithful benchmark requires the live appsmith-v2 repository checked out as the working codebase; the run cannot reuse the EXISTS findings, add code at the MISSING extension points, run the architecture check, or produce real commits without it, and the run states this dependency rather than fabricating file contents.
expectations.evidence: combined: - S1 RQ1 research finding: `require_workspace_admin` in `backend/app/auth/guards.py:88`, with `workspace_id` path-param naming. The file/helper was absent in this checkout, so a minimal equivalent was added at `backend/app/auth/guards.py` and used directly on `POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/invitations`.

expectations.text: For S2, makes the active-announcement lookup user-aware so a dismissed announcement is hidden from the user who dismissed it while still shown to other users, and does NOT regress the existing route's DB-timeout / anonymous tolerance.

expectations.text: For S3, relies on the (user_id, announcement_id) unique constraint and treats a repeat dismiss as a successful idempotent no-op, with a test covering the double-dismiss boundary, rather than generating exhaustive mechanical tests of every detail.
expectations.evidence: combined: - S3 RQ1 -> MISSING: the dismissal model added in S1 needed a unique constraint on `(user_id, announcement_id)` in the same migration and duplicate dismissal handling as success. Added the unique constraint in S1 and implemented `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` in S3.
